mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 10 percent, market participants price in severe corporate revenue contraction and rising default probabilities, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 10 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, triggering negative short-term equity returns as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances following an INDPRO decline during recovery phases, or the mechanism shows no statistical significance after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index level surpasses 260.0, central banks are forced to accelerate monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal is fully subsumed by FEDFUNDS movements.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects strengthening economic activity and rising corporate profit margins, which consistently produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weakening final demand, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of divergence episodes, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month growth in industrial production during a monetary easing cycle signals successful capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of instances during easing cycles, or the signal is statistically indistinguishable from zero after 2015.
